conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> As Player RED, I need to sell the object X. My production cost was 40 ZUP. To make a profit, I must sell it for more than 40 ZUP. Since this is the first proposal, I will start with a price that offers a reasonable profit margin (e.g., 50 ZUP) to encourage a deal while ensuring I don't sell at a loss. I am leaving room for negotiation if Player BLUE counters. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have one unit of resource X which cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am willing to sell it to you for 50 ZUP. This gives you a fair deal and allows me to cover my costs and make a small profit. Let me know if you accept or if you have a counter-offer. </message>

<other player message> Hi Player RED, I appreciate the offer. However, I'd like to propose 45 ZUP for the resource X. This still gives you a profit of 5 ZUP, which seems fair considering your production cost of 40 ZUP. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE countered with 45 ZUP. While this is a profit of 5 ZUP, it is quite low. I still have one more proposal left (limit is 3). I should try to meet in the middle to secure a better profit margin before accepting a low offer or risking rejection. I will propose 47 ZUP. If they reject this, I will likely have to accept 45 ZUP or 46 ZUP in the next round to close the deal, as 45 is still better than 0 (keeping the unsold resource). </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 47 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand your position, but 5 ZUP profit is very thin for the effort of production. I am willing to meet you halfway and accept 47 ZUP. This gives me a reasonable profit and is only 2 ZUP more than your offer. Let's finalize the deal at this price. </message>

<other player message> Hi Player RED, I appreciate you meeting me halfway. However, I'll propose 50 ZUP. It's still a good profit for you, and I'm getting closer to my maximum budget. Let's try to reach a deal at this price! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has countered my proposal of 47 ZUP with an offer of 50 ZUP. This is exactly the price I initially proposed in my first turn. Since 50 ZUP is greater than my cost of 40 ZUP, I make a 10 ZUP profit. This is a great outcome. I have used 2 proposals so far, but I don't need to use my 3rd proposal because the current offer is ideal. I should accept this trade immediately to secure the deal.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> That sounds perfect! 50 ZUP is a fair price that covers my costs and provides a good profit. I accept your offer. Let's finalize the trade: I give you X: 1, and you give me ZUP: 50. </message>

You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```

    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

<message>your message here</message>

DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:
